---
title: "<i>Friendly Work</i> later the <i>Girls\' Quarterly</i>"
---

## <i>Friendly Work</i> later the <i>Girls\' Quarterly</i>

**Type:** periodical

### About

This was a quarterly magazine published by the Girls\' Friendly Society 1883-1917. It changed its name to the <i>Girls\' Quarterly</i> in December 1894. It was edited initially by Mary Elizabeth Townsend, from 1893 by Louisa Fanny Field and from 1900 by Henley I. Arden (<i>i.e.</i> Henrietta Knight).  It was aimed at Members of GFS who were better educated than those who read the sister periodical <i>Friendly Leaves</i>.

### Letters Mentioning This Organization


---

[← Back to all letters](../letters.html)
